cliCommands.description: List configured agents
Manage authentication
Sign in to your Anthropic account
Log out from your Anthropic account
Show authentication status
Inspect auto mode classifier configuration
Print the effective auto mode config as JSON: your settings where set, defaults otherwise
Get AI feedback on your custom auto mode rules
Print the default auto mode environment, allow, and deny rules as JSON
Check the health of your Claude Code auto-updater
Install Claude Code native build. Use [target] to specify version (stable, latest, or specific version)
Configure and manage MCP servers

Add an MCP server to Claude Code. Examples: # Add HTTP server: claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp # Add HTTP server with headers: claude mcp add --transport http corridor https://app.corridor.dev/api/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer ..." # Add stdio server with environment variables: claude mcp add -e API_KEY=xxx my-server -- npx my-mcp-server # Add stdio server with subprocess flags: claude mcp add my-server -- my-command --some-flag arg1
Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
Get details about an MCP server
List configured MCP servers
Remove an MCP server
Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
Start the Claude Code MCP server
Manage Claude Code plugins
Disable an enabled plugin
Enable a disabled plugin
Install a plugin from available marketplaces (use plugin@marketplace for specific marketplace)
List installed plugins
Manage Claude Code marketplaces
Add a marketplace from a URL, path, or GitHub repo
List all configured marketplaces
Remove a configured marketplace
Update marketplace(s) from their source - updates all if no name specified
Uninstall an installed plugin
Update a plugin to the latest version (restart required to apply)
Validate a plugin or marketplace manifest
Connect your local environment for remote-control sessions via claude.ai/code
Set up a long-lived authentication token (requires Claude subscription)
Check for updates and install if available

cliOptions.description: Additional directories to allow tool access to
Agent for the current session. Overrides the 'agent' setting.
Teammate UI color
Teammate agent ID
Teammate display name
Custom agent type for this teammate
JSON object defining custom agents (e.g. '{"reviewer": {"description": "Reviews code", "prompt": "You are a code reviewer"}}')
Enable bypassing all permission checks as an option, without it being enabled by default. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
Append a system prompt to the default system prompt
Read system prompt from a file and append to the default system prompt
Include available plugins from marketplaces (requires --json)

Minimal mode: skip hooks, LSP, plugin sync, attribution, auto-memory, background prefetches, keychain reads, and CLAUDE.md auto-discovery. Sets CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE=1. Anthropic auth is strictly ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper via --settings (OAuth and keychain are never read). 3P providers (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) use their own credentials. Skills still resolve via /skill-name. Explicitly provide context via: --system-prompt[-file], --append-system-prompt[-file], --add-dir (CLAUDE.md dirs), --mcp-config, --settings, --agents, --plugin-dir.
Beta headers to include in API requests (API key users only)
Enable SendUserMessage tool for agent-to-user communication
MCP servers whose channel notifications (inbound push) should register this session. Space-separated server names.
Enable Claude in Chrome integration
Use Claude subscription (default)
Prompt for OAuth client secret (or set MCP_CLIENT_SECRET env var)
Use Anthropic Console (API usage billing) instead of Claude subscription
Use cowork_plugins directory
Load channel servers not on the approved allowlist. For local channel development only. Shows a confirmation dialog at startup.
Bypass all permission checks. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.
Write debug logs to a specific file path (implicitly enables debug mode)
FETCH_HEAD mtime in epoch ms, precomputed by the deep link trampoline
Signal that this session was launched from a deep link
Repo slug the deep link ?repo= parameter resolved to the current cwd
Disable all skills
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high, max)
Pre-populate email address on the login page
Enable auth status messages in SDK mode
Opt in to auto mode
Enable automatic fallback to specified model when default model is overloaded (only works with --print)
File resources to download at startup. Format: file_id:relative_path (e.g., --file file_abc:doc.txt file_def:img.png)
Force installation even if already installed
When resuming, create a new session ID instead of reusing the original (use with --resume or --continue)
Resume a session linked to a PR by PR number/URL, or open interactive picker with optional search term
Automatically connect to IDE on startup if exactly one valid IDE is available
Include partial message chunks as they arrive (only works with --print and --output-format=stream-json)
Run Setup hooks with init trigger, then continue
Run Setup and SessionStart:startup hooks, then exit
Input format (only works with --print): "text" (default), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming input)
Output as JSON (default)
JSON Schema for structured output validation. Example: {"type":"object","properties":{"name":{"type":"string"}},"required":["name"]}
Preserve the plugin's persistent data directory (~/.claude/plugins/data/{id}/)
Run Setup hooks with maintenance trigger, then continue
Maximum dollar amount to spend on API calls (only works with --print)
[DEPRECATED. Use --thinking instead for newer models] Maximum number of thinking tokens (only works with --print)
Maximum number of agentic turns in non-interactive mode. This will early exit the conversation after the specified number of turns. (only works with --print)
Load MCP servers from JSON files or strings (space-separated)
[DEPRECATED. Use --debug instead] Enable MCP debug mode (shows MCP server errors)
Model for the current session. Provide an alias for the latest model (e.g. 'sonnet' or 'opus') or a model's full name (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6').
Disable Claude in Chrome integration
Disable session persistence - sessions will not be saved to disk and cannot be resumed (only works with --print)
Output format (only works with --print): "text" (default), "json" (single result), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming)
Parent session ID for analytics correlation
Permission mode to use for the session
MCP tool to use for permission prompts (only works with --print)
Require plan mode before implementation
Load plugins from a directory for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-dir A --plugin-dir B)
Pre-fill the prompt input with text without submitting it
Alias for --remote-control
Create a remote session with the given description
Start an interactive session with Remote Control enabled (optionally named)
Re-emit user messages from stdin back on stdout for acknowledgment (only works with --input-format=stream-json and --output-format=stream-json)
When resuming, only messages up to and including the assistant message with <message.id> (use with --resume in print mode)
Restore files to state at the specified user message and exit (requires --resume)
Where to declare the marketplace: user (default), project, or local
Use remote WebSocket endpoint for SDK I/O streaming (only with -p and stream-json format)
Use a specific session ID for the conversation (must be a valid UUID)
Comma-separated list of setting sources to load (user, project, local).
Path to a settings JSON file or a JSON string to load additional settings from
Limit checkout to specific directories via git sparse-checkout (for monorepos). Example: --sparse .claude-plugin plugins
Force SSO login flow
Only use MCP servers from --mcp-config, ignoring all other MCP configurations
System prompt to use for the session
Read system prompt from a file
Team name for swarm coordination
How to spawn teammates: "tmux", "in-process", or "auto"
Resume a teleport session, optionally specify session ID
Output as human-readable text
Thinking mode: enabled (equivalent to adaptive), disabled
Create a tmux session for the worktree (requires --worktree). Uses iTerm2 native panes when available; use --tmux=classic for traditional tmux.
Specify the list of available tools from the built-in set. Use "" to disable all tools, "default" to use all tools, or specify tool names (e.g. "Bash,Edit,Read").
Override verbose mode setting from config
Workload tag for billing-header attribution (cc_workload). Process-scoped; set by SDK daemon callers that spawn subprocesses for cron work. (only works with --print)
Disable all enabled plugins
Continue the most recent conversation in the current directory
Enable debug mode
Enable debug mode with optional category filtering (e.g., "api,hooks" or "!1p,!file")
Enable debug mode (to stderr)
Display help for command
Set a display name for this session (shown in /resume and terminal title)
Print response and exit (useful for pipes). Note: The workspace trust dialog is skipped when Claude is run with the -p mode. Only use this flag in directories you trust.
Resume a conversation by session ID, or open interactive picker with optional search term

slashCommands.name: /add-dir
slashCommands.description: Add a new working directory

slashCommands.name: /agents
slashCommands.description: Manage agent configurations

slashCommands.name: /branch
slashCommands.description: Create a branch of the current conversation at this point

slashCommands.name: /bridge-kick
slashCommands.description: Inject bridge failure states for manual recovery testing

slashCommands.name: /btw
slashCommands.description: Ask a quick side question without interrupting the main conversation

slashCommands.name: /chrome
slashCommands.description: Claude in Chrome (Beta) settings

slashCommands.name: /clear
slashCommands.description: Clear conversation history and free up context

slashCommands.name: /color
slashCommands.description: Set the prompt bar color for this session

slashCommands.name: /commit
slashCommands.description: Create a git commit

slashCommands.name: /commit-push-pr
slashCommands.description: Commit, push, and open a PR

slashCommands.name: /compact
slashCommands.description: Clear conversation history but keep a summary in context. Optional: /compact [instructions for summarization]

slashCommands.name: /config
slashCommands.description: Open config panel

slashCommands.name: /context
slashCommands.description: Show current context usage

slashCommands.name: /copy
slashCommands.description: Copy Claude's last response to clipboard (or /copy N for the Nth-latest)

slashCommands.name: /cost
slashCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session

slashCommands.name: /desktop
slashCommands.description: Continue the current session in Claude Desktop

slashCommands.name: /doctor
slashCommands.description: Diagnose and verify your Claude Code installation and settings

slashCommands.name: /effort
slashCommands.description: Set effort level for model usage

slashCommands.name: /exit
slashCommands.description: Exit the REPL

slashCommands.name: /export
slashCommands.description: Export the current conversation to a file or clipboard

slashCommands.name: /extra-usage
slashCommands.description: Configure extra usage to keep working when limits are hit

slashCommands.name: /feedback
slashCommands.description: Submit feedback about Claude Code

slashCommands.name: /files
slashCommands.description: List all files currently in context

slashCommands.name: /heapdump
slashCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

slashCommands.name: /help
slashCommands.description: Show help and available commands

slashCommands.name: /hooks
slashCommands.description: View hook configurations for tool events

slashCommands.name: /ide
slashCommands.description: Manage IDE integrations and show status

slashCommands.name: /init-verifiers
slashCommands.description: Create verifier skill(s) for automated verification of code changes

slashCommands.name: /install-github-app
slashCommands.description: Set up Claude GitHub Actions for a repository

slashCommands.name: /install-slack-app
slashCommands.description: Install the Claude Slack app

slashCommands.name: /keybindings
slashCommands.description: Open or create your keybindings configuration file

slashCommands.name: /login
slashCommands.description: Open or create your keybindings configuration file

slashCommands.name: /logout
slashCommands.description: Sign out from your Anthropic account

slashCommands.name: /loop
slashCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m)

slashCommands.name: /mcp
slashCommands.description: Manage MCP servers

slashCommands.name: /mobile
slashCommands.description: Show QR code to download the Claude mobile app

slashCommands.name: /output-style
slashCommands.description: Toggle a searchable tag on the current session

slashCommands.name: /permissions
slashCommands.description: Manage allow & deny tool permission rules

slashCommands.name: /plan
slashCommands.description: Enable plan mode or view the current session plan

slashCommands.name: /plugin
slashCommands.description: Manage Claude Code plugins

slashCommands.name: /pr-comments
slashCommands.description: Get comments from a GitHub pull request

slashCommands.name: /privacy-settings
slashCommands.description: View and update your privacy settings

slashCommands.name: /release-notes
slashCommands.description: View release notes

slashCommands.name: /reload-plugins
slashCommands.description: Activate pending plugin changes in the current session

slashCommands.name: /remote-control
slashCommands.description: Connect this terminal for remote-control sessions

slashCommands.name: /rename
slashCommands.description: Rename the current conversation

slashCommands.name: /resume
slashCommands.description: Resume a previous conversation

slashCommands.name: /review
slashCommands.description: Review a pull request

slashCommands.name: /rewind
slashCommands.description: Restore the code and/or conversation to a previous point

slashCommands.name: /security-review
slashCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

slashCommands.name: /session
slashCommands.description: Show remote session URL and QR code

slashCommands.name: /skills
slashCommands.description: List available skills

slashCommands.name: /stats
slashCommands.description: Show your Claude Code usage statistics and activity

slashCommands.description: Show Claude Code status including version, model, account, API connectivity, and tool statuses

slashCommands.description: Set up Claude Code's status line UI

slashCommands.name: /stickers
slashCommands.description: Order Claude Code stickers

slashCommands.description: Your 2025 Claude Code Year in Review
